name: stats

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/stats/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/stats/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

<!-- codex:arguments-note v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js -->

> **`$ARGUMENTS` under codex**: there is no argument substitution — the
> argument (e.g. the ticket id) is the text after the `$stats` skill
> mention in the user's message.

# Stats

Run the script. Pass through its output verbatim. It prints three sections (Top influencers / Noise candidates / Never-fired) plus a header line summarizing the window. No agent post-processing.

```bash
node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/synapsys-stats.js" $ARGUMENTS
```

Optional flags (pass through if user provided them):
- `--last=<7d|30d|Nd>` — time window for the aggregation (defaults to `7d`); filters telemetry `.jsonl` files by `mtime`
- `--cwd=<path>` — override the discovery cwd (defaults to `process.cwd()`)
- `--no-color` — disable ANSI color output (useful for piping or CI)

### Sections

- **Top influencers** — memories sorted by `cited` count desc, tiebroken by `fired × cited`. These are the memories that fired AND were referenced in the assistant's response.
- **Noise candidates** — memories with `fired >= 10 AND cited == 0`. Strong signal that the trigger fires too often without being useful.
- **Never-fired** — memories discovered in active stores but with zero `fired` events in the window. Either dead weight or simply not triggered yet.

The script handles empty telemetry directories and malformed JSONL lines (fail-open). Exit code is always `0`. Do not narrate the output; just run and exit.
